senses.definition: a person or thing that saves time by making a process faster or easier
examples: Using a microwave is a timesaver in a busy kitchen.
This new app is a real timesaver for planning trips.

senses.definition: plural of timesaver; items or methods that save time
examples: Kitchen gadgets and apps are common timesavers.
We bought several timesavers to streamline the workflow.

senses.definition: having the effect of saving time; reducing the time needed to do something
examples: The timesaving features reduce the setup time.
A timesaving plan helped us finish earlier.

senses.definition: a scale that shows or measures time in a particular context
examples: The project operates on a tight timescale.
Researchers studied changes over a geological timescale.

senses.definition: plural of timescale; periods over which time is measured in different contexts
examples: Different contexts require different timescales.
Geologists compare events across timescales ranging from minutes to millions of years.

senses.definition: a person who plays for time or acts to gain advantage by delaying action; historically, a person who publicly promotes a cause for personal gain
examples: They called him a timeserver for stalling on the decision.
In politics, some see the candidate as a timeserver who avoids commitments.

senses.definition: plural of timeserver; people who stall or seek personal advantage
examples: The team dislikes two timeservers in the group who avoid responsibility.
The report criticized timeservers who delay crucial decisions.

senses.definition: acting to gain something for oneself; delaying or stalling for advantage
examples: Timeserving behavior can frustrate team members.
The manager accused him of timeserving tactics.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being a timeserver; the tendency to stall for personal gain
examples: Observers noted timeservingness in the negotiation.
The report questioned timeservingness in the administration.

senses.definition: plural form referring to acts or instances of timeserving
examples: The chapter lists several timeservings in corporate culture.
They discussed various timeservings observed during the project.

senses.definition: a property owned by multiple people who have rights to the use of the property at different times
examples: We bought a timeshare in the mountains.
The family divides the year between their two vacation spots in a timeshare program.

senses.definition: to commit to owning or using a property with others on a rotating basis
examples: They decided to timeshare a beach house.
We plan to timeshare with friends for the next five years.

senses.definition: plural of timeshare; properties owned or used by several people on a rotating schedule
examples: Many families buy timeshares for vacation destinations.
Timeshares can be sold or rented when not in use.

senses.definition: the act of owning or using a property with others on a rotating basis
examples: Timesharing is popular among people who vacation infrequently.
They are considering timesharing as a cheaper alternative to buying a second home.

senses.definition: a record of the exact time at which an event occurred
examples: The timestamp on the file shows when it was created.
Each entry has a timestamp for auditing.

senses.definition: to record the time at which something happened
examples: The system timestamps every transaction.
The log file timestamps events automatically.

senses.definition: describing something marked with a timestamp
examples: timestamped data can be verified for accuracy.
The timestamped file shows it was edited yesterday.

senses.definition: having a timestamp recorded on it
examples: All entries are timestamped for traceability.
The timestamped files were archived yesterday.

senses.definition: the act of recording the time of an event or action
examples: Timestamping helps with version control.
Accurate timestamping is important for audits.

senses.definition: plural of timestamp; records showing when events occurred
examples: The log files include several timestamps.
Timestamps help reconstruct the sequence of actions.

senses.definition: a plan showing the times when specific events happen, such as classes or trains
examples: I checked the timetable for the train.
The school timetable lists all classes for the week.

senses.definition: having been planned and scheduled in a timetable
examples: The event is timetabled for Friday.
All activities are timetabled throughout the semester.

senses.definition: plural of timetable; documents showing planned times for events
examples: The university publishes timetables for courses each term.
Trains run according to their timetables.

senses.definition: the process of planning and organizing events or courses in a timetable
examples: Timetabling is usually done by administrators before each term.
Efficient timetabling reduces clashes and gaps.
